Notes: Located along the South Urskoy in the foothills.

wiki_description: Bolgasgrad is a fairly large stanitsa, dominated by Gospodars and proud of its rota of winged lancers. Its boyars have a tradition of strong-minded independence from the central authorities, as well as a history of being replaced with appointees from Kislev.

Castle Alexandronov stands to the North of Erengrad, on the borders of the Troll country. Construction of the castle was originally ordered by the great Tzar Alexandr Njevski. He chose the site guided by a dream in which Boris Ursa, the Forever Tzar of Kislev, directed him to build a great stronghold for Kislev, or else the land would be lost in a war against the greatest evil of all.

Notes: Isolated settlement, not a part of any Oblast
Independent Kislevite kingdom and the land’s major port.
wiki_description: Erengrad, originally called Norvard, is the primary trading port of the northern nation of Kislev. Erengrad is second only to the independent mercantile city-state of Marienburg as a trading port for the northern Old World, and sits at the mouth of the River Lynsk. It is the Kislevite city most likely to be visited and populated by other Old Worlders.
Kislev, also sometimes called Kislev City, originally known as the Imperial trading town of Pelzburg, and later as the Ungol settlement of Dorogo, is the capital city of the Tzardom of Kislev.

Notes: Famous for its sharp and strongly “aromatic” cheeses
Independent Kislevite kingdom, bridge over North Lynsk
wiki_description: Praag, also known as the "Cursed City", is the largest and most heavily fortified city of northern Kislev, earning itself the title of "Bastion of the North". Though rebuilt by the stubborn Kislevites of mostly ethnic Ungol origins who call it home again and again, Praag can never shake off the legacy of its endless sieges and the destruction left behind by the constant assaults of the Norscans and other forces of Chaos.

Notes: Largest town in the Wheatland Colonies
Located in the foothills of the Worlds Edge Mountains
Near the Imperial border where the Urskoy meets the Talabec, ferry
